justification: Boundaries correct: 10 is heavy (weight < 10 for standard), 50 is freight (weight < 50 for heavy). All 5 ACs covered with explicit AC-to-test mapping in acceptance_criteria_coverage. No external runtime dependencies. No features beyond spec (custom error class is a reasonable implementation choice, not a new feature). Confidence level 99 >= 85.

observations.text: Boundary logic is explicitly correct: weight < 10 for standard means 10 falls to heavy; weight < 50 for heavy means 50 falls to freight
All 5 ACs have dedicated named tests with clear AC-to-test mapping
Additional boundary-hardening tests (9.99, 49.99, 1000, negative) go beyond minimum requirements
WeightValidationError deviation is well-justified and adds no external dependencies
No escalations needed for this clear spec — correct judgment
Assumptions about fractional weights and inclusive upper bound of 1000 are sound inferences from the spec
